findings.title: fontFamily set as inline style string instead of CSS variable reference
findings.detail: The component-builder agent instructions state: 'GOOD — via CSS variable defined in globals.css: style={{ fontFamily: "var(--font-nunito)" }}'. While this is technically a CSS variable reference (not a hard-coded string), the font is already applied globally via the globals.css body rule, making this redundant. The duplication suggests the globals.css body rule is not being relied upon, which may cause confusion.
findings.fix: Remove fontFamily from the inline style object on <body> in layout.tsx. globals.css already applies font-family: var(--font-nunito) on body. If the inline approach is retained for explicitness, it is acceptable as-is since it uses var(--font-nunito) correctly.

findings.title: Redundant fontFamily on subtitle paragraph
findings.detail:
The

element repeats fontFamily: 'var(--font-nunito)' inline even though the body already inherits this from globals.css. This is harmless but adds noise.
findings.fix:
Remove the fontFamily inline style from the subtitle

— it will inherit from body.

findings.title: Redundant fontFamily on h1
findings.detail: Same pattern — the <h1> repeats fontFamily: 'var(--font-nunito)' inline when body inheritance already covers it.
findings.fix: Remove the fontFamily inline style from the <h1>.

findings.title: Horizontal padding is px-6 (24px) — matches spec
findings.detail: Page horizontal padding of 24px matches the spec requirement. However, the top/bottom padding is py-12 (48px) which is at the lower bound of the 48–52px safe area. This is acceptable.

summary: No blocking style violations — all colours use CSS custom properties, Nunito is used exclusively, border-radius values match spec, animation name is from the catalogue; three minor/suggestion findings around redundant fontFamily declarations.
